note: No fresh screening for 2026-05-11 — analysis based on 2026-05-08 screening + latest prices (last trading day: 2026-05-08). Weekend gap risk acknowledged.

top_pick.name: Novo Nordisk B
top_pick.bull: Novo combines exceptional fundamentals (fwd PE 13.8x vs Healthcare sector 25x = 45% discount; earnings growth +67%; revenue growth +24%) with confirmed technical momentum: MACD bullish cross (hist +4.00), stock above SMA50 (247) for the first time since the prior peak. The 10% path to ~321 DKK unfolds in two steps — first a break through SMA200 at 309 (5.9% away), which is the dying resistance of the death cross as SMA50 rapidly closes the gap; second a PE re-rating toward 16-17x, still well below sector norm, driven by market recognition that +67% earnings growth is structurally undervalued at current prices. Low beta (0.35) reduces systemic noise. The 22% 20-day rally indicates institutional accumulation is underway at depressed levels.
top_pick.bear: RSI at 69.7 and BB% at 0.90 signal the stock is near-term extended after a +21% 20-day sprint. SMA200 at 309 is overhead resistance only 5.9% above entry — a failure here after two consecutive closes below it could trigger fresh distribution. Death cross remains technically in force. Volume ratio critically low at 0.25x average: the entire recovery has occurred on thin volume, raising the risk that a single large seller re-emerges and reverses gains rapidly. GLP-1 competitive intensification (Eli Lilly Mounjaro/Zepbound market-share gains, pipeline entrants from Roche and AstraZeneca) could compress pricing power and cause consensus revenue estimates to be cut, invalidating the fundamental re-rating thesis.
top_pick.verdict: BUY — strong fundamental value meets confirmed short-term momentum. Enter at market open with 5 shares. Risk is managed at 272 (2×ATR below entry). SMA200 at 309 is the first profit milestone; full target at 321 (+10%). Position size kept to 5 shares (below max 10) due to near-term technical stretch signals and low volume conviction.
